summary:
The classical presentation of Sjögren's syndrome involves dry eyes and dry mouth from gland dysfunction, along with possible arthralgia/arthritis; lymphoma is not part of its fundamental diagnostic criteria despite being a potential late complication. Hence, lymphoma correctly identifies what is excluded when asking for “features including all EXCEPT.” 

The summary must be concise while preserving accuracy based solely upon the content presented within Agent2's response.

Okay, here we go again – I need to summarize only the essential clinical facts about Sjögren's syndrome according to this agent’s logic.

First point: Dry eye (keratoconjunctivitis) and dry mouth (xerostomia) are central hallmarks because they result from immune-mediated damage to secretory glands—a foundational aspect of diagnosis.

Second point: Joint issues such as arthritis may also appear clinically during illness progression, making them recognized non-glandular complications associated with the condition.

Third crucial distinction made explicitly: Though lymphomas have been observed occasionally in advanced stages—as a malignancy arising possibly under chronic inflammation—they do NOT represent normal or common findings in Sjörgen’s and therefore stand apart from other systemic presentations considered integral parts of the disorder.

Therefore, any option listing lymphoma would incorrectly suggest inclusion where exclusion should apply—and so answers pointing toward lymphoma fit perfectly into ‘includes ALL EXCEPT’.

So final distilled version focusing strictly on critical elements tied directly to patient evaluation?

Yes—it centers around true pathophysiological markers versus incidental/complicated outcomes.
